release_notes: Pre-launch reconcile of orphan chrome.exe. Earlier releases occasionally lost their handle on a running chrome.exe (PID-reuse races, bootstrap-window false-dead). When Chromium's named mutex for the user-data-dir stayed owned by such an orphan, the next play silently merged into the orphan and no new window opened — looked like the browser refused to launch. Now launchProfile() runs reconcileOrphans() before spawn: PowerShell + WMI finds any chrome.exe whose command line contains the profile's user-data-dir, sends WM_CLOSE via 'taskkill /PID' (no /T) so Chrome can write Last Session, waits 8s, then taskkill /T /F as a fallback. Orphan from any prior session is auto-cleaned at the next play; no more manual Task-Manager-kill required.
